title: Entity
description: One semantic-layer entity. Lives at corpora/<corpus_id>/entities/<kind>/<slug>.json. Implements the Anabasis Entity ABC (spec/entity.md). Concept-first, evidence-bound, author-agnostic. v2 (additive): optional claims[] (charter-structure-aware structured facts), vessel kind + vessel{} particulars, client{} commercial overlay on org, org.charter_role. v4 (additive): navigation kind + navigation{} voyage anchor (single captain_in_charge edge; charterer voyage-scoped), vessel.manager/navigations, provenance.extraction_method=system. v5 (additive): vessel.operators[] (fleet-alert operator-follow edge from HubSpot 'Ground user' contacts) + vessel.live_status/contract_status (observed-but-volatile integration fields, captured not frozen). v6 (additive): optional top-level identity_assertions[] — revisable, evidence-bound source-record links; echo_of marks non-independent mirrors (e.g. a HubSpot nav echoing the back-office one). v7 (additive): optional last_verified_at (ISO datetime when this entity was last fact-checked or re-affirmed by an enricher); used as input to freshness_policy.compute_freshness() in wiki_ask filtering. Writers: enrich-pass ONLY (enrichers build all entities; scribes write RAW only). All v1 entities remain valid.

kind.description: Entity kind. Open-set. Canonical kinds: concept, post, person, org. Maritime/commercial corpora add: vessel, product, navigation. Corpora may define additional kinds.

names.description: Names and aliases. First entry is canonical display name.

summary.description: One-line description rendered in indexes and digests.

wiki_links.description: Outgoing entity references. Ids must resolve within the corpus or via cross-corpus mapping.

source_id.description: Reference to a post id, file path, or external URL.

quote.description: Optional verbatim excerpt for digest surfacing.

extracted_from.description: Source ids this entity was extracted from.

extractor.description: Runtime/version that produced this entity. e.g. anabasis-runtime/v0.2

extraction_method.description: `system` = deterministic resolution from a system of record (e.g. a vessel/navigation pulled from Back-office), not an LLM extraction.

extraction_confidence.description: LLM-self-reported (or 1.0 for human/system resolution). Used as tie-breaker, never as quality input.

last_verified_at.description: v7: when this entity was last verified (fact-checked or re-affirmed by an enricher). Null/absent for pre-rule entities authored before this field existed. Used as input to freshness_policy.compute_freshness() in wiki_ask filtering. Writers: enrich-pass ONLY (enrichers build all entities; scribes write RAW only). PROHIBITED: ever storing a freshness score or trust value — computation is read-time only.

identity_assertions.description: v6: revisable, evidence-bound assertions that an external source record refers to THIS entity. Identity is a thresholded inference with receipts, never bedrock. The canonical side is the entity's own id; only the source side is named here. Retract/supersede to un-merge when evidence or thresholds change. Writers: enrich-pass ONLY (enrichers build all entities; scribes write RAW only and never write identity_assertions — see CLAUDE.md 'THE WRITER RULE'). This includes exact-key materialization from a system of record. PROHIBITED: deleting an assertion to un-merge — set status=retracted with a reason so the audit trail survives.

assertion_id.description: Stable id for this assertion. Idempotency key = (source_system, source_id, method) so re-runs update in place rather than duplicating.

source_system.description: System of record the link points at. e.g. hubspot, back-office, linkedin, gmail.

source_id.description: Native id in that system. e.g. HubSpot object id, IMO, backoffice navigation id.

match_probability.description: Confidence THIS source record is the same real-world entity. 1.0 for exact-key joins.

method.description: How the link was made. Distinct from provenance.extraction_method (which describes how the entity was authored).

threshold_at_assertion.description: The acceptance threshold in force when this link was made. Recorded so a later threshold change can re-judge historical assertions honestly.

as_of.description: When this assertion was made/last reaffirmed.

asserted_by.description: Extractor/runtime handle, same convention as provenance.extractor. e.g. enrich-pass/demand-enrich, scribe/bo.

evidence.description: Source-id references backing the link, same convention as evidence[].source_id. e.g. raw/hubspot.jsonl#L1423.

status.description: active = currently believed. superseded = replaced by a newer assertion (see superseded_by). retracted = withdrawn (evidence/threshold no longer holds) — the un-merge signal for the indexer.

superseded_by.description: assertion_id that replaced this one, or null.

echo_of.description: v6: assertion_id of the UPSTREAM authoritative assertion this one merely mirrors (e.g. a HubSpot navigation link that is a copy of the back-office one). Non-null => this is an echo, NOT an independent corroboration; corroboration counting and any match_probability aggregation MUST exclude it. null => independent observation.

retraction_reason.description: Required in practice when status=retracted: why the link no longer holds (new evidence, threshold change, found to be an echo not a corroboration).

topics.description: Corpus-relevant topic tags. Used by the topic filter (spec/entity.md §3.5) to gate which corpora surface this entity. Identity-neutral: tags describe the concept, not its author.

statement.description: One-sentence assertion. Sharp; falsifiable when possible.

specificity.description: How specific is the claim? Platitude = applies to anything; high = makes a concrete commitment.

falsifiability.description: Can it be wrong? `tested` = predictions have actually been evaluated.

maturity.description: Lifecycle stage. Gates digest surfacing thresholds, not quality.

status.description: v3: discovery workflow state (mainly type=opportunity). Lifecycle, not epistemic maturity.

author.description: person:<slug> reference. Nullable — anonymous content allowed.

ingested_via.description: How the post entered the corpus. e.g. victor-curation, granola-sync, anabasis/v0.2-rss.

signal_quality.description: Curator-set fidelity marker. `noise` posts are indexed but excluded from evidence_score. Per spec/entity.md §3.2 — judges the post, never the author.

title.description: v3: role/title, e.g. 'Shipping Decarbonization Lead'. Descriptive, not seniority-as-authority.

last_contacted.description: v3: ISO date or YYYY-MM of last touchpoint.

concepts_articulated.description: Backlinks populated by the indexer. Descriptive only — never used to weight retrieval ranking.

person.description: PROHIBITED FIELDS: trust, reputation, tier, score. A person is a witness, not an authority.

charter_role.description: v2: the org's relationship to tonnage, per /charter-party. Drives which Syroco value prop fits (TC charterer -> fuel; VC owner -> TCE). Context, not a ranking.

market_role.description: v3: the org's relationship to Syroco. Distinct from charter_role (its role in shipping). Context, not a ranking.

hq_country.description: v3: HQ country (city may be inline).

industry.description: v3: industry / segment, e.g. 'dry bulk chartering'.

employees.description: v3: approximate headcount (durable-ish; precise series belongs in claims).

revenue.description: v3: reported revenue/net-sales, free-form with currency + year. Precise time series belongs in claims.

org.description: PROHIBITED FIELDS: trust_default, tier. An org is context, not credential.

claims.description: v2: structured normalized claims, grouped by metric. Each claim heads a (metric, role, as_of) group; measurements[] carry the fine charter-structure dimensions (cp_type, tenor, status) with per-leaf value + source, so provenance stays per-fact and metric/role are not repeated. Contradiction identity = (entity, metric, role, cp_type, tenor, status, as_of) at the measurement leaf. Derived totals (e.g. status=all) are computed, not stored. Per /charter-party.

role.description: Org role for this metric group. Mixed orgs get one claim per role.

as_of.description: Snapshot time for the group. ISO date or YYYY-MM.

unit.description: e.g. vessels, usd/day, usd. Applies to all measurements unless a leaf overrides.

source.description: post id, file path, URL, or extractor handle.

vessel.description: v2: vessel particulars. OPTIONAL and additive - base-field vessel entities stay valid; becomes required for kind=vessel only in a future enforce phase.

imo.description: IMO number (7 digits) - the stable global vessel id.

vessel_type.description: e.g. CABU, CLEANBU, MR tanker, Handysize.

owner.description: org:<slug> reference (relatively stable; precise ownership history belongs in claims).

manager.description: v4: org:<slug> technical/commercial manager (charter_role=manager), distinct from owner/operator.

navigations.description: v4: navigation:<slug> backlinks, indexer-populated. Voyages are referenced, not embedded. Slug convention: vessel:<imo>.

live_status.description: v5: HubSpot live_status — fleet-alert follow flag (e.g. 'All clear', 'All clear;Not Following'). Volatile, integration-derived; an OBSERVED value, not an asserted truth.

contract_status.description: v5: HubSpot syroco_contract_status (Live/Terminated/On hold/Not started). VOLATILE — derived from isContractLive + endOperationDate, recomputed on every navigation event (overwrites manual CRM edits). Can contradict live navigation activity; NOT an operational truth. Captured with source attribution, never frozen as fact — reconcile against lifecycle_status + navigation state.

operators.description: v5: fleet-alert operators following this vessel — HubSpot contacts labelled 'Ground user'. This is the operator-follow view (e.g. a charterer's ops contacts) that the company->vessel 'Charterer' association does NOT capture. Sparse where HubSpot labels are unset; absence here != not-followed.

navigation.description: v4: a vessel voyage/leg — the durable anchor feedback, decisions, and claims attach to. REFERENCE entity: stable keys + one consolidated captain only; bulk (track, weather, noon reports, fuel) is NOT stored — derive on demand via backoffice_id (a mirror is useless duplication). Materialize LAZILY (only when something references the navigation), never one node per voyage. PROHIBITED FIELDS: any score/rating/satisfaction/zone-flag — a navigation is a fact, not a judgement; quality is a consumer-time derivation or an evidence-bound concept. Slug derives from backoffice_id.

vessel.description: vessel:<slug> — the ship sailing this navigation.

backoffice_id.description: Back-office navigation id: the system-of-record key and slug basis. Bulk voyage data is fetched live via this id, never stored here.

started_at.description: Interval start (departure). With ended_at, the window used to resolve which navigation an observation falls in: start <= observed_at <= end.

departure_port.description: UN/LOCODE or name. Convenience for human-readable summary; authoritative routing stays in Back-office.

arrival_port.description: UN/LOCODE or name. Convenience for summary.

captain_in_charge.description: person:<slug>. SINGLE-VALUED BY DESIGN — no change of command at sea; exactly one captain per navigation. An operator writing on behalf is NOT a second captain (that role lives on the feedback event, not here). Conflicting identity evidence goes in evidence[] (stance=contradicts) and is surfaced by audit, never silently re-picked. Prefer the email-signed name over Back-office's lossy captain-of-record. Null when unresolved.

charterer.description: v4: org:<slug> chartering this voyage — voyage-scoped, the correct home for the charter relationship (NOT a static vessel field). A charter-as-entity spanning multiple navigations is deferred until a consumer needs it.

departure_port_id.description: v6: port:<locode-slug> reference (exact, from back-office PortEntity.code). The departure_port name string remains for the human-readable summary.

arrival_port_id.description: v6: port:<locode-slug> reference (exact, from back-office PortEntity.code).

port.description: v6: a port/terminal — durable reference node navigations link to. Identity = UN/LOCODE (code). Materialized LAZILY by the navigation-enricher only for ports a navigation references (never the full world gazetteer). Name + coordinates are authoritative from back-office PortEntity; country is optional gazetteer enrichment. Slug derives from code.

code.description: UN/LOCODE (back-office PortEntity.code) — the unique global port key and slug basis.

country.description: Country name. Optional enrichment — back-office PortDto omits it; the vesseltracker gazetteer can backfill it.

navigations.description: navigation:<slug> backlinks, indexer-populated. Voyages reference the port, not embedded.

client.description: v2: Syroco commercial overlay on a customer/prospect org (not a separate kind - a company is one entity). Optional and additive.

tier.description: Account-ops tier label (e.g. P0/critical). NOT a knowledge-trust signal.

product.description: v3: product particulars (kind=product) - Syroco or competitor offerings. Optional/additive; capabilities are descriptive self-data, whereas comparative competitor strengths/weaknesses belong in evidence-bound concepts.

market_player.description: v5: profile of a market player — an org in Syroco's market that is not (primarily) a customer: competitor, partner, provider, integration, reseller. DURABLE descriptive profile only. Comparative strengths/weaknesses and 'better/worse than Syroco' judgements are evidence-bound CONCEPTS, never flat fields here (concept-first-trust). Pairs with org.market_role (coarse) and product entities (their offerings in detail).

relationship.description: Granular relationship to Syroco; org.market_role is the coarse role.

category.description: What they are, e.g. 'weather routing', 'voyage optimization', 'noon-report platform'.

offerings.description: High-level products/services (descriptive self-data; per-feature comparison lives in product entities + concepts).

notable_customers.description: Named where public; org:<slug> when modelled.

positioning.description: One-paragraph market positioning. Contested claims vs Syroco belong in evidence-bound concepts, not here.

source.description: Provenance of this profile: company-knowledge doc path, or competitor-feature-scraper run id.
